health_association.scientific: Measures how health-associated your species composition is based on the GMWI2 model of 155 taxa.
health_association.non_expert: This shows how many of your bacteria are the types typically found in healthy people — think of it as your gut's health fingerprint.

diversity_resilience.scientific: Combined Shannon diversity and guild-level evenness — measures species richness and structural redundancy.
diversity_resilience.non_expert: How many different types of bacteria you have and how well-balanced they are — more variety means your gut can handle stress and recover faster.

metabolic_function.scientific: Composite of 4 CLR diagnostic ratios (CUR, FCR, MDR, PPR) measuring substrate utilization, fermentation efficiency, mucus dependency, and putrefaction pressure.
metabolic_function.non_expert: This shows how well your bacteria are processing food — are they running on the right fuel, converting it efficiently, and producing gentle rather than harsh byproducts.

guild_balance.scientific: Evaluates 6 functional guilds by abundance position within reference ranges and CLR competitive status, weighted by ecological dependency.
guild_balance.non_expert: This checks whether your six bacterial teams are properly staffed — each team has a job, and this score shows if they have enough members to do their work well.

safety_profile.scientific: Absence of dysbiosis-associated taxa (F. nucleatum, S. gallolyticus, P. anaerobius, E-S), extreme guild overgrowth, and BCFA pathway activity.
safety_profile.non_expert: This checks for any red flags — harmful bacteria, extreme overgrowth, or signs of unhealthy fermentation. A high score here means your gut has a clean safety record.

score_drivers.key_note: Your strongest area is safety profile (100% of its potential)

score_summary: Your overall score is 71.6 out of 100. The main areas to focus on are bifidobacteria, inflammatory pressure, fiber processing, microbial diversity, and gut lining protection.

Fiber Degraders.client_interpretation: Your fiber-processing bacteria are below optimal levels but are thriving in the current environment. They're ready to expand when given the right conditions to support better gut health.

Butyrate Producers.client_interpretation: Your butyrate-producing bacteria are slightly low but performing well. These bacteria create important fuel for your gut lining and could benefit from expansion.

Proteolytic Guild.client_interpretation: Your protein-fermenting bacteria are elevated above optimal levels. While not problematic, rebalancing toward other bacterial groups would create a healthier ecosystem.

Cross-Feeders.client_interpretation: Your connector bacteria that help different bacterial teams work together are low but resilient. Expanding this group would improve your gut's overall teamwork and efficiency.

Mucin Degraders.client_interpretation: Your mucus-layer bacteria are in perfect balance, maintaining your gut's protective barrier without overdoing it. This is exactly where you want them to be.

Bifidobacteria.client_interpretation: Your Bifidobacteria are critically low, creating a bottleneck in your gut's fermentation process. These beneficial bacteria produce important fuel that other bacterial teams need to function properly.

strengths.title: Clean safety profile
strengths.text: None of the key dysbiosis-associated bacteria (Fusobacterium nucleatum, Streptococcus gallolyticus, Peptostreptococcus anaerobius, Escherichia-Shigella) were detected at concerning levels. Your gut has a reassuring safety baseline.

strengths.title: Diet-fed ecosystem
strengths.text: Your bacteria are primarily fuelled by what you eat rather than by eroding your gut's protective mucus layer. This is the ecologically stable pattern and reflects a gut that has adequate dietary substrate.

strengths.title: Efficient fermentation
strengths.text: Your gut's fermentation assembly line is running well — intermediate fermentation compounds are being fully converted into beneficial short-chain fatty acids rather than accumulating. This means your bacteria are cooperating efficiently.

challenges.title: Bifidobacteria — absent or critically low
challenges.text: Your bifidobacteria are at only 0.0% and is being outcompeted (CLR -4.72). This is a significant gap — this bacterial group plays an essential role in your gut's fermentation network.

challenges.title: Elevated inflammatory pressure
challenges.text: Your protein-fermenting bacteria are elevated at 6.7% (above the healthy 1-5% range), and your fermentation byproduct profile is shifted toward harsh protein-fermentation compounds. This creates low-grade inflammatory signals in the gut.

challenges.title: Fiber-processing bacteria below range
challenges.text: Your fiber-processing bacteria are at 19.6% — below their healthy range of 30-50%. This is the entry point for the entire fermentation chain, so supporting this group is a primary target.

challenges.title: Moderate diversity
challenges.text: Your species diversity (Shannon 3.10) is in the lower half of the normal range — below the population median of 3.29 but above the 25th percentile. Building more diversity over time is a worthwhile goal and one of the indirect benefits of your prebiotic protocol.

challenges.title: Substrate-starved bacteria — Butyrate Producers
challenges.text: Your butyrate producers are trying to grow (positive competitive position, CLR +0.70) but are below their target range at 8.7%. They're winning the competition but don't have enough substrate to fuel the expansion. This is a substrate-limitation pattern, not a colonization problem.

timeline_phases.label: Settling in
timeline_phases.title: 🌱 Settling in
timeline_phases.body: The probiotic strains in your capsule are adapting to your gut environment — encountering existing bacteria, beginning to colonise, and establishing early positions. Your daily prebiotic sachet is reaching the lower gut, priming the environment before measurable compositional shifts occur. Vitamin C, Zinc, and B-vitamins from your sachet begin supporting immune and energy pathways within days. You may notice small changes in digestion rhythm — this is normal as your gut adjusts.

timeline_phases.label: Early momentum
timeline_phases.title: 🔋 Early momentum
timeline_phases.body: Beneficial bacteria begin expanding as prebiotic fibres consistently supply the substrate they need. Your proteolytic guild start moving toward healthy abundance levels. Short-chain fatty acid production increases as the fermentation chain becomes more active. Omega-3 EPA and DHA from your softgels are building up in cell membranes, typically reaching effective anti-inflammatory concentrations by week 6. Vitamin D3 supports ongoing immune regulation. Many people start noticing improvements in digestion consistency and gut comfort during this window.

timeline_phases.label: Deepening balance
timeline_phases.title: 🛡️ Deepening balance
timeline_phases.body: Guild populations move toward their target ranges. Butyrate output increases as the fermentation chain becomes more complete — your gut lining receives more of the protective compounds it needs for repair. Magnesium Bisglycinate supports deeper sleep and stress resilience as tissue saturation builds. Inflammatory metabolite pressure typically decreases during this phase as the SCFA-to-protein fermentation balance shifts.

timeline_phases.label: Consolidation
timeline_phases.title: ⚖️ Consolidation
timeline_phases.body: The ecological shifts from earlier phases become self-sustaining. A more diverse, well-balanced community is better equipped to hold its gains under normal life pressures — stress, travel, or temporary dietary changes. Botanical adaptogens and the full vitamin-mineral stack have reached their most effective tissue concentrations. Weeks 13–16 are the ideal window for a reassessment sample to measure the full compositional shift.

supplement_cards.name: Probiotic Capsule
supplement_cards.what_it_is: Live beneficial bacteria, selected specifically for your microbiome pattern.
supplement_cards.why: This probiotic blend helps restore balance to your gut bacteria after disruption. Your microbiome analysis shows multiple beneficial bacterial groups are compromised, so these strains work to rebuild a healthier gut foundation.

supplement_cards.name: Omega & Antioxidant Softgel
supplement_cards.what_it_is: Essential fatty acids and antioxidants in an easy-to-absorb softgel form.
supplement_cards.why: This combination supports brain health, reduces inflammation, and provides antioxidant protection throughout your body. The Omega-3s and protective compounds work together to help boost your energy levels and overall wellness.

supplement_cards.name: Daily Prebiotic Powder
supplement_cards.what_it_is: A blend of prebiotic fibres mixed into water — food for your beneficial bacteria, designed for easy daily use.
supplement_cards.why: This prebiotic powder feeds your beneficial gut bacteria to help them thrive. Given your low levels of fiber-processing bacteria, this supports better digestion and gut comfort.

supplement_cards.name: Morning Wellness Capsule
supplement_cards.what_it_is: Targeted vitamins, minerals, and botanical compounds selected for your specific health profile.
supplement_cards.why: This energizing blend combines adaptogenic herbs with essential B vitamins and Vitamin C to combat fatigue. These nutrients work together to support your body's natural energy production and help you feel more alert throughout the day.

supplement_cards.name: Evening Wellness Capsule
supplement_cards.what_it_is: Botanical and functional molecules timed to work with your body's evening recovery processes.
supplement_cards.why: This calming formula helps your mind and body prepare for restful sleep. The relaxing compounds support better sleep quality without causing grogginess the next morning.

supplement_cards.name: Magnesium Bisglycinate Capsule
supplement_cards.what_it_is: Highly absorbable magnesium in bisglycinate form — supports sleep quality, muscle recovery, and stress resilience.
supplement_cards.why: Questionnaire (Sleep: sleep quality 7/10 ≤ 7 + sleep improvement is a stated goal; Sport: Active lifestyle (inferred))

goal_cards.title: Your personal health goal
goal_cards.mechanism: A balanced, resilient gut microbiome provides the foundation for many aspects of health — from energy and metabolism to immunity and mental clarity. Supporting your microbiome creates positive ripple effects across multiple systems.
goal_cards.formula_link: Your formula is designed holistically — the probiotic mix, prebiotic blend, and targeted nutrients all work together to create the conditions your gut needs to function at its best.

goal_cards.title: Boost energy and reduce fatigue
goal_cards.mechanism: The gut microbiome influences energy through multiple pathways: butyrate fuels colonocytes and regulates metabolism, B-vitamins produced by gut bacteria support cellular energy production, and inflammatory load from a disrupted microbiome creates systemic fatigue. A well-balanced gut is the foundation of sustained energy.
goal_cards.formula_link: Your formula targets this through B12 and Folate (essential for energy metabolism), Omega-3 (mitochondrial membrane support), the probiotic and prebiotic blend (restores butyrate production and reduces inflammatory drain on energy), and adaptogenic support to address the stress-fatigue axis.

goal_cards.title: Better sleep quality
goal_cards.mechanism: Sleep and the gut microbiome operate in a bidirectional relationship. Gut bacteria influence circadian rhythm signals, serotonin and melatonin precursor availability, and inflammatory mediators that affect sleep architecture.
goal_cards.formula_link: Your formula targets this through L-Theanine (promotes relaxation without sedation), Magnesium Bisglycinate (supports deep sleep phases), Ashwagandha (reduces HPA-axis activation that disrupts sleep onset), and the LP815 strain (GABA production supports calm states before sleep).

kb_drivers.label: Bloating & Digestive Discomfort
kb_drivers.text: Research consistently links gut fermentation imbalances to IBS-like symptoms. The microbiome pattern we're seeing — particularly the fermentation dysregulation — is a likely explanation for your digestive discomfort.

kb_drivers.label: Tobacco / Smoking
kb_drivers.text: The composition of gut bacteria differs significantly between smokers and non-smokers — smoking creates a distinct microbial environment that tends to favor less beneficial bacterial communities.

kb_drivers.label: Poor Sleep Quality
kb_drivers.text: Bifidobacteria are sensitive to circadian disruption from poor sleep — they thrive best when your sleep-wake rhythm is consistent. Disrupted sleep has been linked to their decline, and their absence may in turn worsen sleep quality by reducing gut-derived calming compounds.

factor_cards.subtitle: Affects 6 bacterial imbalances
factor_cards.explanation: Bloating is a direct symptom of fermentation imbalance — your bacteria are producing more gas than your gut can comfortably handle. This is a functional consequence of the microbiome pattern we're seeing, and it should improve as the fermentation balance shifts.

factor_cards.label: Poor Sleep Quality
factor_cards.subtitle: Affects 3 bacterial imbalances
factor_cards.explanation: Your microbiome has its own daily rhythm. Irregular sleep patterns disrupt this rhythm, affecting bacterial activity and the balance of compounds produced — creating a feedback loop where poor sleep worsens gut health and gut imbalance worsens sleep.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Prioritize diverse fiber sources
lifestyle_recommendations.text: Your fiber-processing bacteria are at 19.6% (well below the 30-50% range), so focus on incorporating 4-5 different types of plant fibers daily from various categories to rebuild this crucial bacterial guild.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Add fermented foods strategically
lifestyle_recommendations.text: With Bifidobacteria at 0% and diversity slightly below median, introduce 1-2 servings of diverse fermented foods daily to seed beneficial bacteria and support your digestion goals.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Moderate protein portions
lifestyle_recommendations.text: Your protein-fermenting bacteria are elevated at 6.7% (above the 1-5% range), so consider reducing portion sizes at meals and spacing protein intake throughout the day to support gut comfort.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Implement consistent meal timing
lifestyle_recommendations.text: Given your moderate stress level and sleep improvement goals, eat your largest meal earlier in the day and maintain regular meal intervals to support circadian rhythm and energy stability.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Increase hydration with fiber
lifestyle_recommendations.text: As you boost fiber intake to support your low gut-lining energy producers (8.7% vs 10-25% range), gradually increase water intake to prevent digestive discomfort and optimize fiber fermentation.
